agents.missingAgentKey: Missing agent key. Open an agent from the chat sidebar or use a valid link.

chat.greeting: What do you want to explore today?
chat.voiceInputNotSupported: Voice input is not supported in this browser
chat.feedbackHelpsImprove: This helps us improve the results next time
chat.ttsNotSupported: Text-to-speech is not supported in this browser
chat.viewAgentTooltipIndividual: View-only: you can’t change this agent’s configuration, but you can connect tools with your own sign-in so they work in your chats with this agent.
chat.viewAgentTooltipServiceAccount: View-only: this organization service agent is locked. You can look around, but nothing here can be edited or reconfigured.

toasts.editComingSoonDescription: We're building out support for edit

chat.label: Chat
chat.description: Think, ask, and reason across your knowledge in a conversational flow

image.label: Image Creation
image.description: Generate images from prompts and company data

agent.label: Agent
agent.description: Multi-step agent with tools—pick a strategy in the toolbar

auto.label: Auto
auto.hint: The system picks the best approach per step—balanced speed and thoroughness.

quick.label: Quick
quick.hint: Favors fast answers and fewer refinement loops. Best for straightforward tasks.

verify.label: Verify
verify.hint: Adds extra checks on facts and steps before finalizing the answer.

deep.label: Deep
deep.hint: Explores more thoroughly with longer reasoning—use when accuracy matters most.

1.text: Find all customer feedback from Slack mentioning latency issues.

2.text: List recurring blockers reported in Jira last month.

3.text: Compare notes from Notion and Slack about the new release.

4.text: Summarize Q3 revenue updates from Jira and Google Sheets

5.text: Show all documents tagged 'Compliance' from Confluence and Drive.

6.text: Show activities updated in GitHub and Jira this week

askMoreQuestionSets: What are the key challenges and how are they being addressed
Can you break down the main strategies involved
What metrics or KPIs are used to measure success
How does this compare to industry best practices
What are the potential risks and mitigation plans
Who are the main stakeholders and what are their roles
Can you provide more details on the implementation process
What tools or technologies are being used
How has this evolved over the past few years
What are the most common pain points teams face
Are there any case studies or examples to reference
How is the team structured to support this
What does the onboarding process look like
How are decisions prioritized and communicated
What feedback mechanisms are in place
What growth opportunities have been identified
How does this impact the overall business goals
What integrations or partnerships are involved
Can you summarize the key takeaways
What would a roadmap for the next quarter look like
How are results reported to leadership
What background context is important to understand this
How does this relate to other ongoing initiatives
What are the short-term vs long-term priorities
Are there any dependencies or blockers to be aware of
What lessons have been learned so far

users.title: Users
users.subtitle: List of all the users who are part of your workspace.

actions.removeUserMessage: Are you sure you want to remove {{email}} from {{workspace}} workspace?
actions.removeSuccessDescription: {{name}} is no longer part of this workspace
actions.unblockTypedConfirmBodyLine1: This will restore sign-in access for {{name}}.
actions.unblockTypedConfirmBodyLine2: Type the user's display name exactly to confirm.

invite.title: Invite Users
invite.emailPlaceholder: Enter an email and press Enter
invite.roleAdminDescription: Can manage all aspects of the workspace
invite.roleMemberDescription: Can view and use the workspace
invite.errorGeneric: Failed to send invitations. Please try again.

groups.title: Groups
groups.subtitle: Create and manage groups to control access and permissions
groups.emptyTitle: You've not created any groups yet
groups.emptyDescription: Groups can make it easier to manage access and permissions across the workspace.

teams.title: Teams
teams.subtitle: Create teams to collaborate, share, and work together
teams.emptyTitle: You don't have any teams yet
teams.emptyDescription: Teams help people organize work and collaborate together. Teams you're part of or create will be shown here.

oauth2.title: OAuth 2.0
oauth2.subtitle: Manage OAuth 2.0 applications that can access your organisation via Pipeshub
oauth2.emptyDescription: Create an OAuth 2.0 app to let third-party integrations access your organisation via Pipeshub.
oauth2.manageComingSoonDescription: Managing OAuth applications from the dashboard is not available yet.

manageApplication.aboutHelper: Use this Client ID in your OAuth flows.
manageApplication.clientSecretsHelper: A client secret is required to authenticate this application with the API.
manageApplication.deleteTypedConfirmBodyLine1: Are you sure you want to delete {{name}}? This action cannot be undone.
manageApplication.deleteTypedConfirmBodyLine2: All data associated with this application will be permanently removed.
manageApplication.deleteTypedConfirmBodyLine3: This includes client credentials, tokens, and any authorization records.
manageApplication.revokeTypedConfirmBodyLine1: Are you sure you want to revoke all tokens for {{name}}? This action cannot be undone.
manageApplication.revokeTypedConfirmBodyLine2: All access and refresh tokens issued for this application will be invalidated immediately. Users will need to re-authorize to obtain new tokens.
manageApplication.suspendTypedConfirmBodyLine1: Are you sure you want to suspend {{name}}? New tokens and usage may be blocked until you activate the application again.
manageApplication.suspendTypedConfirmBodyLine2: Existing tokens may continue to work until they expire or are revoked.
manageApplication.revokeTokensDescription: Invalidates every access and refresh token for this application. This action cannot be undone. Users will need to authorize again to obtain new tokens.
manageApplication.revokeSuccessDescription: All tokens for this application have been invalidated.
manageApplication.deleteApplicationDescription: Permanently delete this OAuth application. Clients using this app will no longer work. This action cannot be undone.
manageApplication.errorLifetimeInvalid: Enter valid lifetimes (whole seconds, at least 1).

create.nextDisabledTooltip: Enter an app name and at least one valid redirect URI.
create.createDisabledScopesTooltip: Select at least one permission scope to create the application.
create.descriptionPlaceholder: A brief description of this application
create.redirectHelper: At least one callback URL is required. HTTPS is required in production.
create.scopesLoadError: Failed to load permissions. Try again.
create.successScreenSubtitle: Store the client secret securely. It will not be displayed again.
create.successDescription: Your OAuth application was created successfully.
create.errorRedirectRequired: At least one redirect URI is required.
create.errorRedirectInvalid: Each redirect URI must be a valid http(s) URL.
create.errorGrantRequired: Select at least one grant type.
create.errorScopeRequired: Select at least one permission scope.
create.errorOptionalUrlInvalid: Optional URLs must be valid http(s) addresses when provided.
create.grantRequiredDescription: At least one grant type must stay selected.

agentBuilder.serviceAccountBadgeTooltip: This agent uses dedicated toolset credentials and full knowledge access for all users.
agentBuilder.convertToServiceAccountTooltip: Convert permanently — dedicated toolset credentials and org-wide knowledge access. Cannot be undone.
agentBuilder.createAsServiceAccountTooltip: Create as a service account with its own toolset credentials and full knowledge access.
agentBuilder.shareWithOrgTooltip: Share this agent with everyone in your organisation.
agentBuilder.serviceAccountShareTooltip: Service account agents are always shared with the organisation.
agentBuilder.saveAsServiceAccountFirst: Save the agent as a service account first, then configure toolset credentials.
agentBuilder.removeNodeFallback: This removes the node and all its connections.
agentBuilder.removeConnectionDesc: This removes the link between two nodes. You can reconnect them from the handles.
agentBuilder.paletteViewOnlyServiceAccount: View only — palette and toolset setup are locked for this organization service account.
agentBuilder.paletteActionBlockedViewOnly: View only: you can’t change this organization service agent or its toolsets from here.
agentBuilder.viewerPaletteDragBlocked: View only: you can’t change the flow from here. You can still authenticate toolsets in Tools below.
agentBuilder.paletteViewerAuthenticateInTools: View only — dragging models, knowledge, and tools onto the canvas is disabled. Use Tools below to sign in to toolsets you need.
agentBuilder.serviceAccountCreated: Service account agent created. Configure each toolset with the key icon in the palette.
agentBuilder.serviceAccountConverted: Agent converted to service account. Configure toolset credentials with the key icon in the palette.
agentBuilder.addToolsHint: Use + to add tools from this toolset
agentBuilder.toolsetNotConfiguredNotify: {{name}} is not configured yet. Ask an administrator to add this toolset.
agentBuilder.toolsetNotReadyNotify: {{name}} is {{reason}}. Configure it before dragging onto the canvas.
agentBuilder.toolsetDuplicateNotify: Only one {{name}} toolset can be on the canvas at a time.
agentBuilder.manageAgentCredentialsTooltip: Manage agent credentials for this toolset
agentBuilder.setAgentCredentialsTooltip: Set agent credentials for this toolset
agentBuilder.svcAcctConvertDesc: This agent will be permanently converted to a Service Account — an independent identity with agent-level toolset credentials, shared with your whole organisation.
agentBuilder.svcAcctCreateDesc: This agent will be created as a Service Account — an independent identity with agent-level toolset credentials, shared with your whole organisation.
agentBuilder.svcAcctConvertNextSteps: Save the agent, then use the key icon next to each toolset in the palette to set agent-level credentials. Existing per-user toolset connections are not transferred.
agentBuilder.svcAcctCreateNextSteps: Save the agent, then use the key icon in the palette to configure credentials for each toolset on this Service Account. Add knowledge, set the model, and refine the flow as needed.
agentBuilder.svcAcctKnowledgeTitle: Your permissions apply to this agent’s attached knowledge only
agentBuilder.svcAcctKnowledgeNote1: Retrieval is limited to the knowledge sources you configure on this agent (connectors and collections attached in the builder) — not everything in the platform.
agentBuilder.svcAcctKnowledgeNote2: Within those attached sources only, search uses your access as the creator.
agentBuilder.svcAcctKnowledgeNote3: Anyone who uses this agent may see answers from content you are allowed to see there, even when their own permissions would not include it.
agentBuilder.svcAcctToolsetsTitle: Toolsets run on this agent’s credentials
agentBuilder.svcAcctToolsetsDesc: Toolsets use one set of credentials for the whole agent, shared by everyone who uses it — not each user’s personal toolset sign-ins.
agentBuilder.svcAcctToolsetsNote1: After you enable a Service Account, configure or update toolset auth with the key icon beside toolsets in the left palette.
agentBuilder.svcAcctToolsetsNote2Convert: Toolset nodes that rely on per-user credentials must be removed from the canvas before you can convert. You can add them again afterwards and attach agent-level credentials.
agentBuilder.svcAcctToolsetsNote2Create: Do not add toolsets that require per-user sign-in until you are ready to supply agent-level credentials for them in the palette.
agentBuilder.svcAcctOrgTitle: This service account is shared with everyone in your organisation
agentBuilder.svcAcctOrgDesc: The agent is available org-wide. All members of your organisation can use this service account agent once it is enabled.
agentBuilder.svcAcctRemoveToolsetsTitle: Remove toolset nodes from the canvas first
agentBuilder.svcAcctRemoveToolsetsDesc: Toolset nodes on the flow still use per-user credentials, which cannot be mixed with a Service Account. Remove them from the canvas, then {{action}} the agent. Add toolsets again afterwards and set agent-level credentials with the key icon in the palette.
agentBuilder.svcAcctConfirmTitle: Confirm you have read the above
agentBuilder.svcAcctAckKnowledge: I understand how attached knowledge and my access apply — including that other users may see answers from content I can access.
agentBuilder.svcAcctAckToolsets: I understand toolsets use one agent-level credential set (palette key icon), not each user’s sign-in, and what that means for this agent.
agentBuilder.svcAcctAckOrg: I understand this Service Account will be available to everyone in my organisation.
agentBuilder.svcAcctCheckboxHintConvert: Check all boxes to enable conversion.
agentBuilder.svcAcctCheckboxHintCreate: Check all boxes to enable creation.
agentBuilder.toolsetCredentialsDesc: Credentials apply to this agent only. All invocations of this toolset through the agent use these credentials.
agentBuilder.fillRequiredFields: Please fill in all required fields.
agentBuilder.oauthConnectedDesc: OAuth is connected for this agent. Use Reconnect to sign in again, or Disconnect to end this session for this agent.
agentBuilder.oauthPendingDesc: Sign in with OAuth in a popup. After you finish, this dialog will detect completion.
agentBuilder.agentCredentialScopeCallout: These credentials are stored for the agent, not for individual users. Anyone who uses this agent shares them when this toolset runs.
agentBuilder.credentialUpdateHint: The agent is authenticated. Enter new values and save to update stored credentials.
agentBuilder.disconnectOAuthDesc: This ends the OAuth session for {{name}} on this agent. The toolset remains on the agent but is not authenticated until you sign in again.
agentBuilder.noCredentialsRequired: No credentials are required for this toolset.
agentBuilder.noCredentialFields: No credential fields were returned for this auth type. If this persists, check the toolset registry schema.
agentBuilder.removeCredentialsDesc: This removes stored credentials for {{name}} on this agent until you configure them again.
agentBuilder.authTimeout: Authentication timed out. Please try again.
agentBuilder.authNotCompleted: Authentication was not completed. Please try again.
agentBuilder.oauthSignInIncomplete: Sign-in did not finish. The window may have been closed before completion — try again when you are ready.
agentBuilder.toolsetOAuthProcessingHint: You can keep this window open until it closes automatically.
agentBuilder.toolsetOAuthBackendRejected: The server did not confirm authentication.
agentBuilder.toolsetOAuthSuccessBody: Authentication succeeded. The window will close automatically.
agentBuilder.toolsetOAuthErrorRetryHint: Authentication failed. Please try again later.
agentBuilder.oauthPopupBlocked: Popup blocked. Allow popups for this site and try again.
agentBuilder.svcAcctEnableFailed: Failed to enable service account mode.
agentBuilder.svcAcctNameRequired: Please enter an agent name before enabling service account mode.
agentBuilder.connectionGroupKnowledge: Group nodes must connect to the agent knowledge handle
agentBuilder.connectionKbToKnowledgeHandle: Knowledge nodes must connect to the agent knowledge handle
agentBuilder.connectionAppToKnowledgeHandle: App nodes must connect to the agent knowledge handle
agentBuilder.connectionLlmToModelsHandle: Model nodes must connect to the agent models handle
agentBuilder.connectionChatInputToInput: Connect chat input to the agent input handle
agentBuilder.connectionToolGroupToToolsets: Tool groups must connect to the agent toolsets handle
agentBuilder.connectionToolsetToToolsets: Toolsets must connect to the agent toolsets handle
agentBuilder.connectionToolToToolsets: Tools must connect to the agent toolsets handle
agentBuilder.connectionAgentResponseToOutput: Connect the agent response handle to chat output
agentBuilder.connectionThroughAgent: Nodes must connect through the agent
agentBuilder.defaultStartMessage: Hello! I am ready to assist you. How can I help you today?
agentBuilder.userToolsetDesc: Authenticate or update credentials for your account. Agents you build will use this connection when you add this toolset to the canvas.
agentBuilder.userCredentialUpdateHint: You are signed in. Enter new values and save to update stored credentials.
agentBuilder.userToolsetOAuthConnected: OAuth is connected for your account. You can clear tokens to sign in again, or remove your credentials.
agentBuilder.userToolsetOAuthPending: Sign in with OAuth in a popup. When you finish, we will refresh your toolset status.
agentBuilder.userToolsetRemoveDesc: This removes your stored credentials for {{name}} until you configure them again.
agentBuilder.userToolsetDisconnectOAuthDesc: This ends your OAuth session for {{name}}. The toolset remains available, but is not authenticated until you sign in again.
agentBuilder.dropNoToolsInToolset: No tools found for this toolset.
agentBuilder.dropConnectorMustEnableNamed: Connector "{{name}}" must be configured and enabled for agents.
agentBuilder.dropConnectorMustEnableApp: Connector for "{{app}}" must be configured and enabled for agents.
agentBuilder.dropEnableConnectorToolGroup: Enable this connector for agents before adding its tool group.
agentBuilder.dropOnlyOneToolGroup: Only one {{type}} tool group is allowed. Remove the existing node first.
agentBuilder.toolsetStatusRegistry: Registry listing — not configured for your workspace
agentBuilder.extractDefaultDescription: AI agent for task automation and assistance
agentBuilder.extractDefaultStartMessage: Hello! I am a flow-based AI agent ready to assist you.
agentBuilder.extractDefaultSystemPrompt: You are a sophisticated flow-based AI agent that processes information through a visual workflow.
agentBuilder.nodeTemplateChatResponseDesc: Sends the reply to the user

oauthConsent.noRedirect: No redirect URL received from server.
oauthConsent.requestHeading: This application is requesting access to:
oauthConsent.legalNotice: By clicking “Allow”, you authorize {{appName}} to access your data as described above.
oauthConsent.outcomeAllowedLine1: The application can access what you approved.
oauthConsent.outcomeDeniedLine1: The application was not granted access.
